sentence_reviews.text: Privacy and governance combine data rights, consent, access control, licensing, retention, audit, risk ownership, and regulatory obligations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF covers privacy, accountability, governance, documentation, risk ownership, and compliance-oriented risk management.
sentence_reviews.notes: Broadly supported, but licensing/retention specifics are synthesized governance terms.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Privacy, governance, licensing & audit is to connect Federated & privacy-preserving learning, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors, Medical imaging & clinical decision support, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports privacy/governance and secure trustworthy AI; AIMA supports database/information-retrieval foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible graph synthesis; downstream list is not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for API gateways, tool registries & secure connectors
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports access control, governance, and secure AI risk management, but not this exact infrastructure relationship.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible but needs an API/security-governance source for direct support.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Medical imaging & clinical decision support
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports privacy and governance in consequential AI contexts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible domain synthesis; no medical-specific source in packet.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Content credentials, signing & provenance
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports governance, documentation, accountability, and traceability concerns relevant to provenance.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible as governance-provenance linkage, though C2PA would be stronger.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Federated & privacy-preserving learning, Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports contextual risk-management limits; AIMA supports data/IR foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic adversary/test-suite language is not specific to privacy governance.

sentence_reviews.text: Privacy, governance, licensing & audit measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Federated & privacy-preserving learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports privacy risk management, but the packet lacks a federated/private-learning technical source.
sentence_reviews.notes: Relationship is plausible; source support is indirect.

sentence_reviews.text: Privacy, governance, licensing & audit measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Databases & information retrieval.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA supports information retrieval/data foundations; NIST supports governance of data privacy and risk.

sentence_reviews.text: Privacy, governance, licensing & audit measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Human evaluation & preference studies.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports human-centered governance, accountability, and stakeholder processes.
sentence_reviews.notes: Common relationship is plausible but not directly necessary.

edge_reviews.notes: Privacy-preserving learning is relevant, but cited support is too broad to establish the edge directly.
Data/IR systems are core governance surfaces for privacy, licensing, and audit.
Human/stakeholder evaluation is a common governance input.
